**Phase context:** Body as constructed percept — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership plasticity.

**Bodily ownership and self-location: components of bodily self-consciousness.** — Conscious Cogn (2013). DOI: 10.1016/j.concog.2013.08.013. PMID: 24025475. Recent research on bodily self-consciousness has assumed that it consists of three distinct components: the experience of owning a body (body ownership); the experience of being a body with a given location within the environment (self-location); and the experience of taking a first-person, body-centered, perspective on that environment (perspective).
